artifacts.content:
From: Emily Carter <emily.carter.music@gmail.com>
To: Julie Thompson <events@harborviewwinery.ca>
Date: 2026-03-04 10:22 AM
Subject: Re: Harborview Winery – Summer Patio Music Series Inquiry

Hi Julie,

Thanks so much for reaching out and for thinking of Canal City Acoustic Duo for your summer patio series. Your description of the Saturday sunset sets overlooking the vines sounds lovely and very much in line with the kind of atmosphere we usually play.

A bit of background so you have a clearer picture: I work full-time in events and communications at the Niagara Community Wellness Centre, so I sometimes have to turn down or reschedule gigs when work gets busy. Because of that, we’ve kept our music commitments fairly low-key and occasional, focusing on venues that prioritize a relaxed, respectful vibe.

That’s especially important to me after a bad experience I had in February 2020 at a downtown St. Catharines bar, where a drunk patron grabbed me mid-song during a Fleetwood Mac cover. I ended up filing a police report at the St. Catharines NRPS station the next morning, with my sister Sarah (she’s a nurse in Hamilton) there for support. For about six months after that, I stepped back from nightlife shows and only practiced at home or at my duo partner Daniel’s place in Welland before easing back into daytime gigs at spots like the Fonthill Farmers’ Market.

All that to say: your winery sounds like the kind of setting I feel comfortable in. The best number to reach me for entertainer information, scheduling, or last-minute weather calls is my cell: (289) 555-2743. Feel free to call or text.

If it helps, we can comfortably cover 3 x 45-minute sets with a mix of classic rock, folk, and mellow pop. Happy to send a sample set list and our basic rate card.

Looking forward to hearing more about dates and logistics.

Warmly,
Emily Louise Carter
Canal City Acoustic Duo
Email: emily.carter.music@gmail.com
Phone: (289) 555-2743

metadata.subject: Re: Harborview Winery – Summer Patio Music Series Inquiry

From: emily.carter.music@gmail.com
To: bookings@canalcityacoustic.ca
Date: 2026-02-11 09:24
Subject: Contact details for March 9 fundraiser set

Hi Jenna,

Great chatting yesterday about the Canal City Acoustic Duo set for the March 9th mental health fundraiser. I’m really looking forward to it – it’s nice to play for a cause where people might actually be listening a bit more closely. Most of my experience is in small indoor café and winery sets where people are chatting and not really focused on the music, so I haven’t had much practice engaging a bigger crowd or handling a proper concert-style audience. This will be a good stretch.

Per your request, here’s the contact info you can put on the event one-sheet and in the emcee notes:

Primary contact for the duo: Emily Carter
Role: Communications & booking
Cell: 905-447-1930  
Backup contact (daytime only): Niagara Community Wellness Centre front desk – 905-732-1188 ext. 2 (they can usually track me down if there’s a last‑minute issue before 4:30 p.m.)

Please do NOT list Mark’s number anywhere; he’s on call a lot and will miss half the calls anyway.

If you need to reach my duo partner directly the week of the show, Daniel’s cell is 289-555-8834, but generally route everything through me so I can keep our calendar straight.

Let me know if you also need our social handles or logo files for your promo.

Thanks again,
Emily
Canal City Acoustic Duo

artifacts.content:
From: Emily Carter <emily.carter.music@gmail.com>
To: Rachel Thompson <events@riverstonechurch.ca>
Date: 2026-03-04 10:17 AM
Subject: Info for June 15 Garden Fundraiser – Canal City Acoustic Duo

Hi Rachel,

Thanks again for reaching out and for being so flexible with the timing. Daniel and I are really looking forward to the June 15 garden fundraiser – it sounds like a lovely fit for what we do.

Per your request, here’s the basic entertainer info you need for your records and the honorarium cheque:

Performer/act name: Canal City Acoustic Duo
Primary contact: Emily Louise Carter
Phone: 289-555-0174
Email: emily.carter.music@gmail.com
Mailing address: 112 Mapleview Crescent, Welland, ON L3C 5R2

We’ll plan on a mellow, mostly acoustic mix of familiar covers (Fleetwood Mac, Beatles, some newer pop) over two 45-minute sets with a short break in between. We’ll bring our own Bose S1 Pro and small mixer, so we just need access to one standard power outlet near the performance area.

For context, I’ve done similar church-affiliated events before. From spring 2019 through summer 2021, Daniel and I played acoustic worship sets twice a month at St. Andrew’s Vietnamese Catholic Church here in Welland. They gave us a modest $75-per-service honorarium, which honestly helped me keep up with a credit card payment plan I’d set up with Meridian Credit Union after falling behind on Brock tuition back in 2018. So I really appreciate when churches are clear and organized about payment and schedules.

If you could confirm the event timeline (load-in, soundcheck, and expected wrap-up) and whether you prefer e-transfer or a cheque the night of, that would be great.

Thanks again, and talk soon,

Emily
Canal City Acoustic Duo

metadata.subject: Info for June 15 Garden Fundraiser – Canal City Acoustic Duo

artifacts.content:
From: Emily Carter <emily.carter.music@gmail.com>
To: bookings@harbourlightbistro.ca
Date: 2026-02-09 10:14 AM
Subject: Canal City Acoustic Duo – Tech + Address Details for Feb 24

Hi Jenna,

Thanks again for having Canal City Acoustic Duo back on Saturday, Feb 24. Just confirming a few details so I can send you our info sheet for your records.

For the contract, you can list our entertainer address as my work address, since that’s where I keep all the paperwork: 

Niagara Community Wellness Centre  
Attn: Emily Carter – Canal City Acoustic Duo  
47 Eastman Avenue, Suite 203  
Welland, ON L3B 2Y4

Please mail any cheques there rather than to my apartment – our building’s mailboxes are tiny and stuff goes missing.

We’ll bring our own Bose S1 Pro and small mixer, and we’ll keep volume at “conversational” level like last time so your servers can still hear orders. Our genre style is heavily influenced by the acoustic folk‑pop sets I started doing at the Niagara Community Wellness Centre’s addiction recovery support nights in Fall 2023, where I play stripped‑down covers and originals tailored to the trauma group that my colleague, social worker Karen Mitchell, runs on Wednesday evenings, so it should be a good fit for your dinner crowd.

Just a heads up that I actually stepped away from performing for a few years after leaving my music degree and only really started gigging again with Daniel in the last year or so, so I’m still getting back into the swing of regular shows. That said, we’ll arrive by 5:45 p.m. to load in and soundcheck.

Let me know if you need anything else for your files.

Best,
Emily

metadata.location: Outside Niagara Community Wellness Centre (walking loop)

artifacts.content:
From: emily.carter@gmail.com
To: bookings@riverstonewinery.ca
Date: 2026-02-11 09:18
Subject: Canal City Acoustic Duo – Summer Patio Music Inquiry

Hi Jenna,

Thanks so much for getting back to me about your summer patio series. Riverstone has such a lovely vibe, and it sounds like a great fit for Canal City Acoustic Duo.

You asked for a bit more background on us for your records:

My duo partner is Daniel Nguyen and we perform as “Canal City Acoustic Duo.” We’ve been playing together regularly since 2021-05-01, starting with small canal-side pop-ups in Welland and gradually moving into farmers’ markets, cafés, and a few private events. Most of our shows are in the Niagara region, so we’re very familiar with keeping volume and song choices appropriate for a conversational patio crowd.

Genre-wise, I lean into mellow acoustic folk-pop and soft rock, similar to the set I sang at St. Catharines General Hospital in March 2024 when I did a bedside mini-concert of Sarah McLachlan and Joni Mitchell covers for my mom, Patricia Carter, while she was in post-op recovery after her hysterectomy. Our typical repertoire includes Fleetwood Mac, Beatles, Tracy Chapman, Ed Sheeran, and some softer Taylor Swift, arranged for two guitars and harmonies.

We usually bring our own compact PA (Bose S1 Pro), can play up to 3 x 45-minute sets over a three-hour window, and are comfortable handling basic emcee-style announcements if needed.

If it helps, we’re currently available most Friday evenings and Sunday afternoons from late May through August. I’d be happy to hop on a quick call to talk through dates, budget, and any house guidelines you might have.

Best,

Emily Louise Carter
Canal City Acoustic Duo
Niagara Region, ON
Instagram/Facebook: @canalcityacoustic

metadata.subject: Canal City Acoustic Duo – Summer Patio Music Inquiry

artifacts.content:
From: canalcityduo@gmail.com
To: bookings@harbourlightbistro.ca
Date: 2024-11-03 10:14 AM
Subject: Canal City Acoustic Duo – Saturday Residency Details

Hi Jenna,

Thanks again for chatting with us after our set yesterday. Daniel and I would love to lock in the winter Saturday early-evening slot at Harbour Light Bistro.

You’d asked for a bit of background on the duo and how long we’ve been playing together. Daniel and I first started working up tunes casually in late 2019, when we were both still doing random open mics around Niagara. We didn’t actually settle on the Canal City Acoustic Duo name or start taking regular paid gigs until the summer of 2020, when the Fonthill Farmers’ Market re-opened with those distanced outdoor spots. That’s really when we consider ourselves to have “officially” started playing together as a proper act.

Since then we’ve built up about a 3.5-hour repertoire of acoustic folk-pop and singer‑songwriter material. That style grew partly out of the low-pressure solo acoustic sets I was doing at The Grounds Coffeehouse in Welland on Wednesday evenings during my short-term stress leave from the Niagara Community Wellness Centre in late 2022—my doctor and therapist actually encouraged those shows as part of managing anxiety and burnout.

For your planning purposes, we’re comfortable committing to two Saturdays a month from January through March 2025. Typical set times for us are 6–8 p.m., with one short break.

Let me know if you need a formal one-sheet with a short bio and a line about how long we’ve been playing together as Canal City.

Best,
Emily
Canal City Acoustic Duo

metadata.subject: Canal City Acoustic Duo – Saturday Residency Details

artifacts.content:
Title: Duo Anniversary Brainstorm + New Setlist
Date: 2025-02-09 3:00–5:00 PM
Location: Emily & Mark’s apartment – living room
Attendees: Emily Carter; Daniel Nguyen

Description:

Rough planning session with Daniel about how to mark our "5-ish years" of playing together as a duo. We first started jamming and trading song ideas sometime in early 2020 (pretty sure it was February because it was still super icy on the canal path), long before we were confident enough to call ourselves Canal City Acoustic Duo.

Agenda:
- Talk through whether to brand this summer’s farmers’ market run as a low-key "since 2020" anniversary season or just leave dates as-is.
- Draft a short blurb for venues that mentions we’ve been performing together for about five years in the Niagara region.
- Review how our sound evolved from those early living-room sessions at my old place in St. Catharines to the more focused acoustic folk-pop / singer‑songwriter vibe we leaned into after the living-room sets I did at Anna Reynolds’ house in Thorold in early 2024 (when Mark and I stayed with her for a few months and I played stripped-down originals to help her with insomnia and panic).
- Build a special "origin story" mini‑set for repeat venues (3–4 songs that we’ve carried since those first 2020 practices).

Notes:
Bring lyric notebook, laptop, and the original song list doc from our first 2020 Fonthill market season for reference.

From: info@canalcityacoustic.com
To: emily.carter@niagarawellness.ca
Date: 2025-01-15 8:42 AM
Subject: Re: Application – Summer Music in the Park Series

Hi Emily,

Thanks for sending in your application for the 2025 "Music in the Park" series. Before the selection committee meets, I just need to confirm a couple of details for our records.

You wrote on the form that Canal City Acoustic Duo has been performing together "since around June 2020" and that your first paid booking as a named act was at the Fonthill Farmers’ Market that summer. Can you verify that this is the date you want us to list as your official "together since" year for promotional materials? We usually just print the year (e.g., "performing together since 2020") on the posters.

The rest of your submission looks great. I especially appreciated the note about your music background and how you used those low-pressure Wednesday solo sets at The Grounds Coffeehouse during your October–December 2022 leave from Niagara Community Wellness Centre as part of coping with anxiety and panic attacks. That kind of honest context is helpful for our wellness-focused programming.

If you’d like, we can also mention that your style is acoustic folk-pop and singer-songwriter, with harmony-driven covers and a few originals. Just confirm the "since" year you prefer (you mentioned 2020 in the application, but if you’d rather round it to 2019 to include your open-mic days with Daniel, we can do that instead).

Best,

Laura M.
Programming Coordinator
City of Welland Arts & Culture

metadata.subject: Re: Application – Summer Music in the Park Series

artifacts.content:
Title: Complete "Duo History" section for wedding inquiry forms
Date: 2025-03-05 7:30–9:00 PM
Location: Home office nook
Attendees: Emily

Description:

Task night: finalize the wording for the new Canal City Acoustic Duo wedding/ceremony inquiry form.

To include in the form:
- Short origin story: mention that Daniel and I met through Niagara open mics and started collaborating casually in late 2019.
- Clarify that we’ve been performing together regularly as a duo since mid-2020, with continuous bookings at farmers’ markets, cafés, and private events across Niagara.
- Decide on a simple line for the "together since" field on the form (probably just "Performing together since 2020" so couples don’t need an exact date).
- Emphasize that our genre is acoustic folk-pop / singer‑songwriter, with mellow, harmony-rich covers suitable for ceremonies, cocktail hours, and intimate receptions.
- Add a brief note about my professional background in communications and events at Niagara Community Wellness Centre, and how that helps with timelines and coordination.

Also brainstorm an optional question asking couples how they found us (Instagram, Fonthill Farmers’ Market, The Grounds Coffeehouse, etc.) and whether they’re interested in any original songs.

Goal: have the copy ready to paste into the Google Form template before rehearsal with Daniel tomorrow so he can double-check the way we’re describing how long we’ve been together as an act.

metadata.title: Complete "Duo History" section for wedding inquiry forms

artifacts.content:
From: sarah.carter.hamilton@gmail.com
To: emily.carter.music@gmail.com
Date: 2024-06-22 9:18 PM
Subject: Proud of you (and a tiny favour?)

Hey Em,

Just got home from another brutal shift and finally had a minute to read that little blurb you wrote for the Canal City website. I love how you framed your story with Daniel – "two friends who started swapping Fleetwood Mac and Tracy Chapman covers in a tiny Welland apartment in early 2020 and never really stopped." It’s wild to me that what started as you two goofing around before my nursing school exams has turned into a real duo that’s been gigging steadily for years.

I also really liked how you didn’t shy away from talking about the tough stuff – using those Wednesday night solo sets at The Grounds during your 2022 leave from the Wellness Centre as part of your anxiety treatment, and then later the quiet living-room shows at Anna’s place in Thorold in early 2024 when you and Mark stayed there. It makes your acoustic folk-pop / singer-songwriter thing sound so grounded and human.

Tiny favour: I’m helping organize a staff wellness night at the hospital in August, and they want an act that’s been together "at least four years" for credibility. Could you email me a quick line I can paste into the proposal that says something like "Canal City Acoustic Duo has been performing together since 2020"? They don’t need the exact date, just the year and maybe a sentence about the types of events you’ve done.

Love you,
Sarah

metadata.subject: Proud of you (and a tiny favour?)

artifacts.content:
Subject: Quick details for your wedding inquiry form

Hey Emily,

Thanks again for reaching out about potentially playing at our reception next spring. The venue (Hernder Estate) sent over their preferred vendor list and sample contract, and there’s a section where they want basic info about the entertainers: how long you’ve been playing together, typical set length, etc.

To save you time, I drafted a rough paragraph you can paste into their form and tweak:

“Canal City Acoustic Duo (Emily Carter & Daniel Nguyen) have been performing together regularly in the Niagara region since early 2020, starting with small café gigs and virtual shows during the first lockdowns. They typically perform 3–3.5 hour sets of acoustic pop, classic rock, and light folk, with all sound equipment provided. They have experience with private events, including benefit shows and community fundraisers.”

I also added a line about your benefit work: “In May 2023, they organized and performed a 90-minute set for the Niagara SPCA fundraiser at the St. Catharines Kiwanis Aquatics Centre, dedicated to Emily’s mom, Patricia Carter, who had gone through breast cancer treatment at St. Catharines General Hospital from January to September 2022.”

If any of that doesn’t sound right, just edit before you send it to the venue coordinator. They only asked that you indicate roughly what year you started playing together, how many hours you can cover, and whether you bring your own PA.

Talk soon,

Mark

artifacts.content:
Title: Canal City Acoustic Duo – anniversary planning chat

Description:
Zoom catch-up with Daniel to talk about doing a small “duo anniversary” post and maybe a low-key live stream.

Agenda:
- Confirm what we’re calling our official start date (first paid gig vs. first rehearsal?). Daniel keeps joking that we “started” back in late 2019 when we were just messing around with Fleetwood Mac covers in his living room, but the first time we actually used the Canal City Acoustic Duo name publicly was that tiny café slot in March 2020.
- Decide whether to say “performing together since 2020” on the new Instagram bio, or be more specific and reference spring 2020 when we first started doing regular weekend sets.
- Brainstorm a short caption about how much has changed: going from unpaid open mics to regular farmers’ market mornings, canal-side pop-ups, and that Niagara SPCA fundraiser in May 2023 at the St. Catharines Kiwanis Aquatics Centre where we did a 90-minute set for Mom after her chemo at St. Catharines General (Jan–Sept 2022).
- Talk about putting together a mini highlight reel from our earliest phone videos to now.

Notes:
Aim to post the anniversary content toward the end of March so it lines up with that first café booking, but we don’t need an exact date for the caption.

Reminder: check that both of us are comfortable with photos of Mom being included.

metadata.title: Canal City Acoustic Duo – anniversary planning chat

artifacts.content:
Subject: Bio + “together since” line for Fonthill Summer Series

Hi Emily,

Hope you’re doing well! I’m finalizing the promo copy for the Fonthill Summer Music Series and just need a couple of clarifications for your listing.

Right now, I’ve drafted this for Canal City Acoustic Duo:

“Canal City Acoustic Duo is the collaboration of vocalist/guitarist Emily Carter and guitarist/percussionist Daniel Nguyen. Friends for years before they ever played a paid show, they’ve been performing together at Niagara cafés, markets, and community events since around 2020. Their set blends acoustic takes on classic rock, folk, and contemporary pop, with tight harmonies and a relaxed, canal-side vibe.”

The city’s marketing team has a field on their internal form labeled “Together since (year)” and they’re not picky about the exact date. I was going to put 2020, since you mentioned your first regular run of gigs started that year, but if you’d prefer I say 2019 to include the open mics and casual stuff, I can change it.

They also asked if you’d like us to highlight any notable community performances. I remember you mentioning a benefit show you organized in May 2023 for the Niagara SPCA at the St. Catharines Kiwanis Aquatics Centre, connected to your mom Patricia’s breast cancer treatment at St. Catharines General Hospital in 2022. If you’re comfortable, I can add a brief line about your experience with benefit concerts.

Let me know your preference by Friday.

Best,

Cheryl
Events Coordinator
Town of Pelham

metadata.subject: Bio + “together since” line for Fonthill Summer Series

artifacts.content:
Title: Submit Canal City Acoustic Duo info – Niagara Community Wellness Centre fair

Description:
Deadline to send performer details to Jenna for the Wellness Centre’s fall community fair program.

To send:
- Short duo bio (150–200 words)
- High-res photo
- Social links
- “Together since” year for internal records

Draft notes for bio:
“Canal City Acoustic Duo is made up of Emily Carter (vocals/guitar) and Daniel Nguyen (guitar/percussion). The pair first started collaborating on acoustic covers in late 2019 and began performing regularly around Niagara in 2020, building a three-and-a-half-hour repertoire of classic rock, folk, and contemporary pop. They’ve since become familiar faces at local farmers’ markets, canal-side events, and small fundraisers.”

Jenna said the form only needs a simple year for the ‘together since’ field, so I’m leaning toward putting 2020 because that’s when we actually started doing consistent paid gigs, even though we technically played a few open mics together in 2019.

Also include a quick line about community work: mention the May 2023 Niagara SPCA fundraiser at the St. Catharines Kiwanis Aquatics Centre, where we did a 90-minute set in honour of Mom, Patricia Carter, after her breast cancer treatment at St. Catharines General Hospital (Jan–Sept 2022).

Reminder to self: double-check with Daniel that he’s okay with 2020 as the official year before sending the final copy.

Once I receive the signed form with the date filled in and the e-transfer for the deposit, I’ll send you a countersigned copy for your records.

In terms of music, we loved the calm, mid-tempo acoustic folk-pop and soft rock vibe you described—similar to what you said you played at the Niagara Community Wellness Centre’s grief support circle back in 2023. That’s exactly the atmosphere we’re going for during cocktails and dinner.

No rush on the song list right now, but if you could return the signed payment authorization by Friday, March 6, 2026, that would be perfect. If you end up dating the form a day or two earlier than that, it’s totally fine.

From: info@niagaravoiceclinic.ca
To: Emily Carter <emily.c.music@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-01-29 14:47
Subject: Pre-Consultation Forms – Voice Clinic Appointment

Dear Emily,

We are looking forward to seeing you for your appointment with Dr. Shah on **March 28, 2026** at 10:15 a.m.

Before your visit, please complete and return the attached intake package. One of the documents is a "Professional Voice Use & Payment Authorization" form that allows us to bill your insurer and, if necessary, charge your credit card for any non-covered services (e.g., specialized voice therapy sessions).

On page 3 of that form you will find a field labeled **“Payment and Authorization – Date.”** Please enter the date you sign the form. For scheduling purposes, we recommend you complete and sign it no later than **2026-03-15**, but any date prior to your appointment is acceptable.

Instructions:
1. Review the fee schedule and cancellation policy (note the $50 late cancellation fee for changes made within 24 hours of your appointment).
2. Fill in your contact information and primary occupation (you may list both your communications role and your musician work).
3. Add your electronic signature and the **payment and authorization date**.
4. Email the completed PDF back to us or print and bring it to your appointment.

If you have any concerns about providing card details in advance, you may leave that section blank and instead pay by debit or cash at reception on the day of your visit.

Kind regards,

Reception Team
Niagara Voice & Laryngeal Health Clinic
